verses.commentary: Now in Goshen. Formal announcement.
Chose five brothers. Strategic selection. Audience with king.
Expected question. Prepared answer.
Temporary stay requested (but becomes permanent). Need explained.
Pharaoh to Joseph.
Generous grant. Best land offered. Goshen confirmed.
Jacob meets Pharaoh. Blesses the king. Greater blesses lesser (Hebrews 7:7).
Jacob's age remarkable.
Pilgrimage (megurim, מְגוּרַי), sojourning. 130 years old. Abraham lived 175 (25:7). Isaac lived 180 (35:28). Honest assessment.
Jacob blesses Pharaoh again on departure. The patriarch maintains priestly authority even before Egypt's throne.
District of Rameses. As Pharaoh directed. Settlement complete. Property given.
Full support. Family care.
Famine severe. Desperate situation. Widespread crisis.
Payment for grain. Money for food. Revenue to palace.
Why should we die? Money is gone. Crisis deepens. Currency exhausted. They plead for food, but have nothing left to trade.
I will sell you food in exchange for your livestock, since your money is gone. Bring livestock. Since money gone. Second stage. Barter system. Livestock for food.
Complete exchange.
That year ended. Cannot hide. Money gone.
Buy us and our land for food. Total submission. They choose slavery over starvation. Why perish when we can serve? Crisis reorganizes the entire society.
Famine too severe.
Harsh reality. Later generations will experience same enslavement.
Had enough food. Didn't sell land. Priestly exemption.
Here is seed. Plant the ground. Seeds for planting.
When crop comes. Eighty percent kept. Sustainable system.
Gratitude expressed.
System continues.
Acquired property. Israel prospers. Fulfillment of promise. God blesses them in Egypt.
Same number Joseph was when separated (17 years old). 147 total.
Promise kindness and faithfulness. Summons Joseph. Ancient oath gesture (like Abraham's servant, 24:2). Kindness (chesed, חֶסֶד) and faithfulness. Burial request. Not Egypt.
Family tomb at Machpelah (23:19). Joseph agrees.
Joseph swore. Israel worshiped leaning on staff. Oath required. Joseph swears. Israel worships God. Leaning on staff. Faith in God's promises.
